Sección
Environmental voices
WELCOME: What are we going to do?
What are we going to do?

We are going to deal with the topic of the environment using English as the language of communication.

Throughout the four sessions you are going to do:

  • An individual podcast using Vocaroo.com/ mobile phone
  • A group infographic using digital tools such as Genially or Canva.

The main objective is to improve your speaking production and reflect about  the environmental problems affecting the Earth planet.
Text and Tasks
SESSION 1:Environmental problems
Video 1: Climate Change 101 with Bill Nye

We are going to analyse the current state of our world throughout two different prespectives. At the end of this session, you will have to choose a topic for your podcast.

Let's watch the first video:
Text and Tasks
Video 2: BBC 6 Minute English
Now listen to this second perspective from the BBC about how to help the environment:
Text and Tasks
Understanding Environmental Perspectives

Duración:
30 minutos
Agrupamiento:
Pairs or small groups

1. Identifying key Information:

From video 1 (National Geografic): List three main causes of global warming mentioned by Bill Nye.
From video 2 ( BBC 6 Minute English): Summarize the two main solutions discussed in the conversation.


2. Vocabulary building

Choose five new expressions or words related to the environment that you found interesting ( e.g. carbon footprint, fossil fuels, sustainable). You must use at least three of them in your future infographic.

3. Critical Thinking

In pairs, discuss and answer the following question:

" Which environmental challenge mentioned in the videos do you think is the most urgent in our city/town/ region? Why?
Text and Tasks
Check your understanding
University of Auckland

Although more often used in formal testing situations MCQs can be used as a testing tool to stimulate thought and discussion on topics students may feel a little reticent in responding to. When designing a MCQ test consider the following:
  • What learning outcomes are the questions testing
  • What intellectual skills are being tested
  • What are the language skills of the audience
  • Gender and cultural issues
  • Avoid grammar language and question structures that might provide clues

When building an MCQ consider the following:
  • Use phrases that learners are familiar with and have encountered in their study
  • Keep responses concise
  • There should be some consistency between the stem and the responses
  • Provide enough options to challenge learners to think about their response
  • Try to make sure that correct responses are not more detailed than the distractors
  • Distractors should be incorrect but plausible
Introduzca la pregunta. La pregunta debe ser clara y no ambigua. Evite las premisas negativas ya que estas tienden a confundir a los estudiantes.

SESSION 2: Language and planning
Designing your infographic

Goal: Visualizing Solutions

    In this session, you will work with your partner to design an infographic that summarizes the environmental challenge you analyzed in the previous session.

    Instructions:

    Step 1 - Planning: Review your notes. Select three causes and three solutions.
    Step 2 - Design: Use Canva or Genially.
    Step 3 - Content: Include a title, icons, and at least three new keywords (e.g., carbon footprint, sustainable...).
    Note: Be ready to present your draft at the end of the session!
Text and Tasks

Useful vocabulary for your infografic:

ENVIRONMENTAL PROBLEMS SOLUTIONS AND ACTIONS
Global Warming Renewable Energy
Carbon Footprint To phase out
Deforestation Sustainable
Pollutants Eco-friendly
Text and Tasks
CHECK YOUR VOCABULARY
Provide instruction on how the cloze activity should be completed. Default text will be entered if there are no changes to this field.
Read the following sentences and fill in the missing words based on the vocabulary table above.
University of Auckland
If this option is checked, submitted answers with different capitalization will be marked as incorrect.

To protect our planet, it is essential to promote sustainability in our daily lives. We must try to reduce our carbon footprint by using public transport or recycling. If we care about the environment, we should avoid single-use plastics and support renewable energy.
If this option is set, each word will be marked as the learner types it rather than all the words being marked the end of the exercise.

Enter the text for the cloze activity in to the cloze field by either pasting text from another source or by typing text directly into the field.

To select words to hide, double click on the word to select it and click on the Hide/Show Word button below.

More than one possible answer can be defined enclosing them with pipes (|). I.e.: |dog|cat|bird|

If left unchecked a small number of spelling and capitalization errors will be accepted. If checked only an exact match in spelling and capitalization will be accepted.

For example: If the correct answer is Elephant then both elephant and Eliphant will be judged "close enough" by the algorithm as it only has one letter wrong, even if "Check Capitalization" is on.

If capitalization checking is off in the above example, the lowercase e will not be considered a mistake and eliphant will also be accepted.

If both "Strict Marking" and "Check Capitalization" are set, the only correct answer is "Elephant". If only "Strict Marking" is checked and "Check Capitalization" is not, "elephant" will also be accepted.

To protect our planet, it is essential to promote sustainability in our daily lives. We must try to reduce our carbon footprint by using public transport or recycling. If we care about the environment, we should avoid single-use plastics and support renewable energy.

Cloze exercises are texts or sentences where students must fill in missing words. They are often used for the following purposes:

  1. To check knowledge of core course concepts (this could be a pre-check, formative exercise, or summative check).
  2. To check reading comprehension.
  3. To check vocabulary knowledge.
  4. To check word formation and/or grammatical competence.

If your goal is to test understanding of core concepts or reading comprehension

Write a summary of the concept or reading long enough to adequately test the target's knowledge, but short enough not to induce fatigue. Less than one typed page is probably adequate, but probably considerably less for young students or beginners.

Select words in the text thatare key to understanding the concepts. Thesewill probably be verbs, nouns, and key adverbs.Choose alternatives with one clear answer.

If your goal is to test vocabulary knowledge

Write a text using the target vocabulary. This text should be coherent and cohesive, and be of an appropriate length. Highlight the target words in the text. Choose alternatives with one clear answer.

If your goal is to test word formation/grammar:

Write a text using the target forms. This text should be coherent and cohesive, and be of an appropriate length. Remember that the goal is not vocabulary knowledge, so the core meanings of the stem words should be well known to the students.

Highlight the target words in the text. Provide alternatives with the same word stem, but different affixes. It is a good idea to get a colleague to test the test/exercise to make sure there are no surprises!
Cloze Activity
Enter any feedback you wish to provide the learner with-in the feedback field. This field can be left blank.
"Great job! You have mastered the key vocabulary for this unit. Now you are ready to use these words in your infographic!"

🛠️ Tools & Getting Started


We recommend using one of these free online tools. Both have thousands of Infographic Templates ready for you:

Canva: The most popular and easiest to use.Click here to see examples on Canva

How to start: Search for "Infographic" in the search bar, pick a template you like, and double-click on the text to change it.


Genially: Great if you want to add "interactive" buttons (pop-up text, videos).Click here to see interactive templates on Genially.

How to start: Choose "Create Genially" > "Infographics".
💡 Quick Step-by-Step:

Sign up: Use your school email or Google account.
Collaborate: If you work in pairs, one person creates the file and uses the "Share" button to invite the partner. Now you can both edit at the same time!
Download: When finished, download your work as a PDF or PNG image.
Text and Tasks
SESSION 3: Podcast and infographic
Final products: Infographic and pódcast

In this final stage, you will combine your visual design skills with your speaking ability. Remember: the infographic is a collaborative team effort, but the podcast is an individual task.

  • Step 1: Finish your Infographic (Teamwork)
    Working in your pairs/groups, complete your design in Canva or Genially. 
    Make sure it includes the "Environmental Problem", "Solutions", and at least one interactive element (like a button or a video link).
    Download it as a PDF or copy the public link.
  • Step 2: Record your Podcast (Individual Task)
    Every student must record their own audio ( 2-3 minutes). You cannot record it with your partner!
    Content: a specific environmental issue in your town or region. 
    Tool: Use your phone’s recorder or Vocaroo.com (very easy to use!).
  • Step 3: Submission on Teams
    Go to our Class Teams > Assignments > "SdA: Environmental Solutions".
    Together: One member of the team uploads the Infographic.
    Individually: Each student must upload their own Podcast file.
Text and Tasks
The challenge: Think globally, act locally

For this individual task, you will record a podcast episode focusing on a specific environmental issue affecting our local area or region (your town, a local natural park, or our city).

Content Guidelines

Your podcast should last between 2 and 3 minutes and include the following sections:

1. Introduction: Present the local issue. Where exactly is it happening? (e.g., pollution in a local river, lack of recycling bins in your neighborhood, or the impact of tourism on our local beaches).
2. Current Situation: Explain how this problem is affecting our community today. Use B2 connectors like Furthermore, Consequently, or As a result.
3. Personal Perspective: Share your opinion. Why do you think this has been neglected? Who is responsible?
4. A Local Solution: Propose one realistic and practical solution that our local government or we, as neighbors, could implement.
5. Closing: End with a powerful "Call to Action" to inspire your listeners.


Technical Requirements

Format: MP3 or WAV file.
Duration: 120 - 180 seconds.
Language Focus: You must use at least 3 specific terms from our vocabulary list and at least 2 conditional sentences (e.g., "If we don't clean the river, the local ecosystem will collapse").
Text and Tasks
IMPORTANT
Please, review the [Assessment of the SdA] section to check the rubric before you submit your work.
Assesment of the SdA

🔎 Content: Research and solutions. 💬 Language: Grammar and vocabulary. 🎙️ Oral Fluency: Podcast quality. 🎨 Design: Infographic layout.

Rúbrica de ejemplo (4x4)

CONTENT&RESEARCHDeeply researched local issue with a very creative and realistic solution. (2.5)Clear local issue identified with a practical and logical solution. LANGUAGEExcellent use of B2 vocabulary, connectors, and complex structures like conditionals. (2.5)Good use of vocabulary and connectors with very few errors. (1.75)Basic vocabulary used. Some errors in B2 structures (conditionals or connectors). (1.50)Limited vocabulary. Frequent grammar errors that hinder communication. (0.5)
ORAL FLUENCY(pódcast)Very natural flow, excellent intonation, and clear pronunciation. Sounds professional. (2.5)Clear and steady pace. Easy to follow and understand throughout. (1.75)Some hesitation or "reading" tone, but generally clear and audible. (1.50)Very hesitant, unclear pronunciation, or audio quality is too poor. (0.5)
DESIGN: infographicHighly professional layout. Perfect balance between text and visual elements. (2.5)Well-organized, attractive, and easy to read. Good use of icons and images. (1.75)Basic design. Some parts are slightly cluttered or hard to follow. (1.50)Unorganized, lacks visual appeal, or text is difficult to read. (0.5)

Other Contents
ASSESSMENT CRITERIA

These are the criteria for your mark, taking into account that your final mark includes the Podcast, Infographic, and the written task from the Extra Practice section:

Podcast & Infographic: 70% of the final mark.
Extra Practice & Games: 30% of the final mark.
Text and Tasks
Text and Tasks
SESSION 4: Reflection and peer feedback
Reflexction and peer feedback

Sharing our Vision

Now it is time to share your hard work with the rest of the class. We will have a "Gallery Walk" and a listening session.

The Process: You will present your infographic to your classmates and we will listen to some selected podcast clips.
Peer Feedback: Using a "Two Stars and a Wish" technique, you must provide feedback to at least two classmates. Write down two things you loved about their work (the stars) and one constructive suggestion for future projects (the wish).
Text and Tasks

Think about your learning process during this unit:

Take a moment to reflect on your progress during this unit. Answer the following questions in your learning diary:

1. Which specific B2 vocabulary words or structures (like conditionals) do you feel more confident using now?
2. What was the most difficult part of researching a local environmental issue?
3. How has your digital competence improved by creating an infographic and a podcast?
Text and Tasks
⚠️  Please, review the [Assessment of the SdA] section to check the rubric before you submit your work.
Text and Tasks
Essential vocabulary

TERM DEFINITION
Biodiversity The variety of plant and animal life in a particular habitat or ecosystem.
 Carbon footprint The total amount of greenhouse gases produced to directly and indirectly support human activities.
Greenwashing  When a company gives a false impression of how its products are more environmentally sound than they actually are.
Landfield  A site for the disposal of waste materials by burial. It is the oldest and most common form of waste disposal.
Renewable energy  Energy from a source that is not depleted when used, such as wind or solar power..
Sustainability The ability to be maintained at a certain rate or level without depleting natural resources for future generations.
Wildfires  Large, destructive fires that spread quickly over woodland or brush, often exacerbated by climate change.
Energy-efficient  Using less energy to provide the same level of performance, comfort, or convenience.
To phase out  To gradually stop using something. (e.g., The government aims to phase out single-use plastics).
Wind turbine A wind-driven turbine for generating electricity
Solar pannel A device that changes energy from the sun into electricity:
Eco-friendly  Not harmful to the environment; designed to have little or no damaging effect on the ecosystem.

Final Challenge: Applying what we learned.

Duración:
60 minutes
Agrupamiento:
Individual work


Activity: Choose one of the environmental problems we discussed in the unit (e.g., Deforestation, Plastic Pollution, or Greenwashing). Research a real-life example of this problem occurring in the place where you live,in Asturias or in Spain.

Instructions:

1.Write a short summary (around 120-150 words) describing the problem and suggesting one possible solution using the vocabulary from our Glossary.

2. Format: Use a word processor like Microsoft Word or Google Docs.

3. Submission: Save your document as a PDF and upload it to the Virtual Classroom (Teams) for assessment.
